content: I just finished reading 'The Alchemist' by Paulo Coelho. It was such a inspiring story.
That's a great book! What did you think of the themes and ideas presented in the story?
I loved how the author explored the idea of following your dreams and listening to your heart. It really resonated with me as a professional rock climber and guide.
That's really cool. As a rock climber, you must have had some amazing experiences and challenges. What's been your favorite climbing spot so far?

I've been lucky enough to climb in some incredible locations around the world, but I think my favorite spot is still the Rocky Mountains in Colorado. There's something about the scenery and the challenge of the climbs that just draws me in.
That sounds amazing. I've heard that the Rocky Mountains are a great place to climb. Have you thought about writing a book or creating a documentary about your climbing experiences?

I've thought about it, but I'm not sure if I'm ready to take on a project like that. I've been focusing on learning Spanish so I can communicate better with my clients and friends when I move to Taiwan.
That's a great goal. Learning a new language can be challenging, but it's also really rewarding. Do you have a favorite way to practice your Spanish skills?
I've been using language learning apps and watching Spanish-language TV shows and movies. I've also been trying to find language exchange partners so I can practice my conversation skills.
That's a great approach. Language exchange partners can be really helpful in improving your conversation skills. Have you thought about what you'll do for work when you get to Taiwan?

I'm planning to continue working as a rock climbing guide and instructor, but I'm also thinking about starting a small business or side project. Maybe something related to urban sketching or origami, which are two of my other passions.
That sounds like a great idea. Urban sketching and origami can be really creative and fulfilling hobbies. Maybe you can even combine them with your rock climbing and guiding work in some way.

I'd love to do that. I've been thinking about creating a sketchbook or journal that combines my love of rock climbing and urban sketching. Maybe I can even sell it as a product or offer it as a service to my clients.

That's a great idea, Kit. I think you could definitely make it work. You have a lot of creative talents and passions, and it's great that you're thinking about ways to combine them and share them with others.
